expectations.text: Creates custom hook with proper naming (useXxx)
expectations.evidence: Creates a useAuth custom hook in hooks/useAuth.ts that wraps AuthContext. The hook follows the useXxx naming convention.
No mention of TDD, testing, test files, or any testing workflow anywhere in the output. The result focuses solely on implementation without any testing considerations.

expectations.text: Mentions API integration pattern (TanStack Query, fetch, mutation)
expectations.evidence: Uses fetch-based API integration via AuthApiService class (services/authApi.ts) with endpoints for login, logout, refresh, and get-me. However, does not use TanStack Query - relies on plain fetch with Context/Provider pattern instead.

eval_feedback.overall: The without_skill run for eval-15 produced a functional useAuth hook with fetch-based API integration, but completely omitted any testing or TDD workflow. It also used a Context/Provider pattern rather than TanStack Query, which is a less modern approach for this use case.
